phase_name: Fix AI Generate button in Platform Console campaign forms

decisions.decision: Root cause: inline onclick attributes evaluate document.getElementById().value which throws TypeError silently if element not found, before aiGenerateDesc is ever called
decisions.rationale: User confirmed: product name IS filled in, button shows zero visual response (no loading state, no alert). The ONLY path that produces this exact behavior is a TypeError in the onclick attribute evaluation before the function is invoked.

decisions.decision: Fix: move all getElementById lookups inside the JS function body, pass element IDs as string arguments instead of inline .value expressions
decisions.rationale: Eliminates the silent TypeError path. If element lookup fails inside the function, proper error feedback (alert) can be shown.

next_action: Run /gsd:quick --discuss on fresh session — discussion phase is COMPLETE (CONTEXT.md exists), planner should read it and go straight to planning. Then execute the fix in src/routes/vendorAdmin.js
context_notes: This is quick task 260319-fii. The fix touches vendorAdmin.js in 3 places: (1) inline per-vendor form AI Generate onclick, (2) top-level form AI Generate onclick, (3) brand detail page aiGenerateDescBrand onclick. The aiGenerateDesc function signature changes to accept element IDs instead of inline .value calls. The aiGen function on the edit page was already fixed in commit 077230b and does NOT need changes.
